name: LangGraph
description: Low-level graph-based orchestration framework for building stateful, long-running agents with durable execution, human-in-the-loop workflows, and persistent memory

description.value: Low-level graph-based orchestration framework for building stateful, long-running agents with durable execution, human-in-the-loop workflows, and persistent memory

first-release.comment: Announced and released on 2024-01-22 as part of LangChain's Week of Jan 22 2024 release notes

latest-release.comment: langgraph v1.1.8 released 2026-04-17; weekly release cadence

pricing-model.comment: Core library is MIT-licensed free OSS; LangSmith has a free tier; LangGraph Platform offers paid cloud (Plus) and enterprise self-hosted / hybrid deployments

covered-phases.comment: LangGraph is a low-level general-purpose agent orchestration framework and does not prescribe SDLC phases; users define what their graphs cover

methodology-style.comment: First-party langgraph-supervisor and langgraph-swarm packages implement multi-agent hierarchies; plan-and-execute is a documented common pattern; core value is the harness/runtime rather than a prescribed methodology

opinionated.comment: Explicitly positioned as a low-level orchestration framework that does not abstract prompts or architecture; users define node/edge topology freely

ceremony-level.comment: Minimal scaffolding: a langgraph.json config plus graph modules; no mandatory review gates, specs, or phase rituals

mechanization-level.comment: A compiled graph runs end-to-end via a single invoke()/stream() call; langgraph CLI runs the Agent Server locally

hitl-checkpoints.comment: interrupt() function pauses execution at user-defined checkpoints and awaits Command(resume=...); opt-in rather than prescribed

approval-gates.comment: No prescribed approval gates in the default configuration; any interrupt() gates are user-defined

autonomy-level.comment: Default graph execution is fully autonomous from invoke to completion; any human checkpoints are opt-in via interrupt()

rollback-support.comment: Time-travel via checkpoints supports rewinding/branching graph state, but there is no built-in mechanism to revert generated code, files, or other external side effects

supported-agents.comment: LangGraph is itself an agent runtime rather than a workflow driven by an external coding agent; the supported-agents concept does not meaningfully apply

editor-integrations.comment: LangGraph Studio (now LangSmith Studio) is a web IDE for visualizing, interrupting, and debugging graphs; no first-party VS Code or JetBrains extension. langgraph CLI also available

model-agnostic.comment: Works with any LLM provider via LangChain chat-model integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, AWS Bedrock, Azure, Ollama, etc.); does not require LangChain

mcp-support.comment: First-party langchain-mcp-adapters package wraps MCP servers as LangChain/LangGraph tools via MultiServerMCPClient (stdio, HTTP, SSE, WebSocket)

artifact-formats.comment: Primary non-code artifact is langgraph.json (config for dependencies, graphs, env, checkpointer, store, http); the actual workflow is code (Python/TS)

artifacts-covered.comment: Does not produce or maintain SDLC artifacts (specs, plans, ADRs, release notes); the framework's artifacts are graph code and the langgraph.json config

project-layout.value: Minimal: `langgraph.json` at project root declares graphs, dependencies, env, checkpointer, and store config; graph modules are referenced from the config (e.g., `./my_graph.py:graph`). No prescribed directory structure.

artifacts-in-git.comment: langgraph.json and graph code live in the repo; only checkpointer state (SQLite/Postgres) and traces (LangSmith) live outside git

language-agnostic.comment: Framework itself is available in Python and JavaScript/TypeScript (LangGraph.js); agents built on it can target any target-language codebase via tools

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Primary enforcement is instructional via node functions and edges; mechanical hooks via interrupt(), pre/post-model hooks, and MCP interceptors

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Node and edge topology is user-defined; the framework does not prescribe phases or enforce ordering beyond the user's graph

artifact-validation.comment: langgraph.json is validated against a schema by the CLI; State schemas (TypedDict / Pydantic) are validated at runtime

memory-implementation.comment: Checkpointer persists per-thread state to InMemory/SQLite/Postgres/CosmosDB; Store interface provides cross-thread KV plus optional vector semantic search

persistent-memory.comment: Checkpointers persist graph state between sessions by thread_id; Store persists shared memory across threads

project-context-files.comment: Does not use or produce CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursorrules style project context files; agent behavior lives in code and prompts

cost-tracking.comment: LangSmith (optional integration) aggregates prompt/completion token counts and costs per trace and per child run; no built-in hard budget limits

run-history.comment: LangSmith stores structured traces with per-node timing, I/O, and token usage when LANGSMITH_TRACING=true

telemetry-opt.comment: Tracing is opt-in via LANGSMITH_TRACING and LANGSMITH_API_KEY environment variables; nothing is uploaded unless explicitly configured

custom-templates.comment: LangGraph Templates (ReAct, RAG, Memory Agent, Data Enrichment, etc.) are user-forkable; users can also author their own graphs and state schemas freely

hooks.comment: interrupt() pauses execution; pre/post-model hooks and middleware patterns exist; MCP adapters expose interceptors for modifying tool calls

sub-agents.comment: First-party langgraph-supervisor and langgraph-swarm packages support role-based multi-agent composition; users define agent roles, tools, and handoff logic

marketplace.comment: Official LangGraph Templates gallery (Python + JS) accessible via LangGraph Studio and GitHub; community-maintained awesome-LangGraph index; no paid third-party marketplace

install-mechanism.comment: `pip install langgraph` for Python; `npm install @langchain/langgraph` for JS/TS; langgraph-cli available via pip or npx

setup-time.comment: `pip install langgraph` plus a small graph module runs a hello-world in minutes; LangGraph Platform deploys take longer

self-hosted.comment: Core library runs entirely on user infrastructure; LangGraph Platform also offers self-hosted and hybrid enterprise deployments

requires-account.comment: Not required for the core OSS library; a LangSmith account is needed only for tracing, Studio, and LangGraph Platform

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs at docs.langchain.com covering concepts (graphs, state, persistence, HITL, streaming), platform/deploy, CLI, templates, and API reference; parallel Python and JS documentation

active-maintenance.comment: v1.1.8 released 2026-04-17 (within 4 days of research on 2026-04-21); weekly release cadence

example-projects.comment: Official templates (ReAct, RAG Research Agent, Memory Agent, Data Enrichment, Retrieval Agent, Full-stack Chatbot) in Python and JS; broad community examples via awesome-LangGraph

community-channel.comment: LangChain Forum (primary Q&A), LangChain Community Slack, and GitHub Discussions on the langgraph repo
